title: ID Agents Control Center Wiki
description: Human-readable wiki and editable UI schematic for the Control Center. Each page.body field is the prose wiki article; the desktop app still reads page nav metadata for sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility, and keeps the structured sections as supporting implementation detail.

editContract.hotReload: The desktop app polls this file while running. Local edits to body prose, nav labels, icons, order, visibility, page descriptions, tabs, tiles, fields, dropdowns, controls, displays, and routing notes appear in the UI without a rebuild.
editContract.pathOverride: Set IDCTL_WIKI_PATH=/absolute/path/to/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json to drive the UI from a different local schematic file.
editContract.routeRule: A page only becomes navigable if its route is implemented in idctl-desktop/src/renderer/App.tsx. Unknown routes are shown in the Wiki but skipped by the sidebar.
editContract.body: pages[].body is markdown prose for operators. Keep each article grounded in current renderer behavior, explain when to use the page, and call out routing or safety boundaries. Do not remove nav, route, component, sourceFiles, or structured sections because those continue to drive the live UI and schematic detail.
editContract.releaseDrift: scripts/check-wiki.mjs compares this appVersion and updated marker with idctl-desktop/package.json, validates page sourceFiles, and fails when a page source file changes without docs/CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json changing in the same worktree diff. Set IDCTL_WIKI_DRIFT_BASE=<ref> to compare committed branch changes against a base ref as well.
editContract.releaseNaming: Operator-facing release notes, ops/deployer handoffs, and ops-team content-ops release-cycle wiki updates should say IDACC release / idacc release, not IACC release / iacc release. IDACC means ID Agent Control Center.

global.shell: Electron desktop app. Renderer calls window.idagents.call(method, ...args); preload sends IPC to main; main either handles app-local methods or forwards manager methods through bridge.ts and ManagerClient.
global.state: useFleet in src/renderer/store.ts keeps the live snapshot: connection, manager URL, active team, coordinator, agents, teams, events, inbox, unread chat count, all-team agents, and refresh functions. Renderer mutations also emit coarse sync-domain invalidations so mounted Dashboard, Work, Inbox, Projects, and Health views refresh together after plans, goals, loops, schedules, tasks, project, org, and agent-capability changes. The fleet primary lead is locked to the default team; other teams can still choose coordinators that the default primary delegates to and default-team validators review. The external Brain dashboard's /fleet-report now mirrors IDACC's all-team manager polling contract: /teams, then /agents per team, with Brain-cache fallback and drift warnings instead of stale cached counts. Brain Health approval triage now fresh-reads the approval row and honors backend confirmation-required responses before mutating queued memory, fact, instruction, or graph state. Brain Learning keeps passive report refresh automatic but runs eval replay/vector comparison only from an explicit operator refresh. Brain Agents keeps live row status authoritative while holding same-name SLA, unfetched telemetry, and bare controller fallbacks as ambiguous or unknown until scoped telemetry exists; Brain Graph now labels itself as a read-only GET view, marks agent/entity statuses as Brain graph snapshots rather than live lifecycle authority, and overlays live Fleet status only for unambiguous agent matches. HR Manager Build surfaces preloaded-team coverage and default return-path readiness before non-default team onboarding. Capabilities previews and stamps local skill catalogs before Brain sync writes, and its Skills tab opens each Brain dashboard tab through a fixed whitelist. Identity and Computer Use legacy authority cards now provide scoped selection/copy helpers without auto-promoting old bare-name records. Settings defaults the manual backend form to Ollama, exposes a first-run readiness checkpoint for manager connection, provider routing, local runtime, synced backends, and guarded setup actions, shows coordinator status as read-only with an HR Manager routing handoff, non-embedded Chat keeps its coordinator marker read-only, and Health lead markers also hand routing edits to HR Manager. Focused HR routing handoffs from Dashboard, Health, Settings, and hidden Chat now use the App shell target teams:route so coordinator and org-sync review lands directly on HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync instead of the generic Structure tab.

pages.body:
# Dashboard

Dashboard is the daily command view. It combines lead chat for the selected team with all-team coordination, active task state, recent events, and hierarchy context so an operator can steer work without losing fleet awareness.

## Use it for

- Send a request to the selected team's lead while watching coordination across every running team.
- Check whether work is moving through the leadership chain before opening the deeper Work or Health pages.
- Read the event stream when you need fast context on what just changed.

## Current guardrails

Dashboard is now observe-first by default. Command-palette project and org sync entries open Projects or HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync instead of mutating tracker or hierarchy state immediately. The shortcuts drawer shows Probe all, Refresh, and owner-page handoffs first; direct project sync and org sync are kept under Advanced direct shortcuts, and direct org sync previews affected agents, goal files, brain writes, planned rebuilds, and deferred busy rebuilds before apply. Primary fleet-lead changes are no longer exposed as a Dashboard selector; use HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for the routing diff preview.

## Watch for

The Dashboard is not the fleet roster grid. Agent lifecycle controls, probes, and full roster inspection live on Health through AgentTable. If the question is "who is running?" use Health; if the question is "what should the lead do next?" start here.

pages.body:
# Chat

Chat is the full conversation workspace behind Dashboard lead chat. It owns sessions, target selection, project focus, attachments, generated images, live traces, resumable polling, unread state, and saved history.

## Use it for

- Continue or inspect a conversation with complete message history.
- Attach files, paste context, generate image context, or focus the dispatch on a tracked project.
- Review the behind-the-scenes trace for tool, file, and delegation activity after a reply lands.

## Routing note

Chat is currently embedded into Dashboard and hidden from normal sidebar navigation. Dashboard uses the same dispatch path but locks the target to the selected team's coordinator; direct Chat use is for contextual or deep-link flows that need the full session surface. Non-embedded Chat can choose the message target, but its coordinator star is read-only. Use HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for coordinator and primary-lead changes.

pages.body:
# Inbox

Inbox is the operator decision queue. It separates direct blocker questions and manager inbox items from the general event feed so work that needs a human answer is visible and actionable.

## Use it for

- Answer option questions surfaced by agents or Work blocker scans.
- Reply to manager inbox items that are explicitly waiting on the operator.
- Dismiss stale prompts after deciding they no longer need a response.

## Operating rule

Use Inbox to resolve blockers, then return to Chat or Work for continued execution. It is intentionally narrower than a conversation view: the goal is to clear pending decisions and update the linked task state where the manager provides a task reference.

pages.body:
# Work

Work is the execution surface for plans, task cards, schedules, loops, and dream review. It turns operator intent into tracked manager work and gives the team a place to move items through planning, assignment, review, and completion.

## Start here when

- A request needs to become tasks, not just a chat reply.
- You need to triage unassigned work, assign a fleet objective, or surface blockers for a human decision.
- You want scheduled follow-up, recurring loops, or reviewed idea drafts.

## Tab guide

- Plans: structured plans and dispatch handoff.
- Tasks: Kanban execution, review lanes, scoped assignment, and blocker surfacing.
- Schedule: team-aware heartbeats and supervision reminders.
- Loops: recurring objectives.
- Dream: proposed ideas or simulations that require review before action.

Dragging cards now fresh-reads the task, lane overlay, and dependency overlay before it writes a review lane or mapped manager status. Assignment and re-dispatch also fresh-read live status, owner, and team before overwriting ownership or sending another background request. Plans now fresh-read live brain-plan title/status/mtime before status writes, blocker questions, or dispatch, and draft-plan update/promote/delete paths block if the opened draft changed. Goals now fresh-read the opened goal before field edits, AI refine saves, and delete; the goal driver merges metadata into the latest saved goal and Autopilot prompts show global active-goal scope. Loops now keep a baseline for saved chains and block stale save, run, schedule, or delete actions before overwriting a newer chain. Schedule now fresh-reads heartbeat and check-in rows before update, pause, remove, close, or bulk cleanup. Dream prevents duplicate nightly schedules for the same team/agent and fresh-checks reports before delete. Assignment can dispatch to one team, selected teams, team leads, or every active agent, grouping manager plan creation by target team so owners stay scoped correctly. Schedule heartbeats use team-qualified targets, so cross-team agents and manager-level heartbeats remain visible and editable from the Schedule tab.

pages.body:
# Projects

Projects is the local workspace registry. It tracks folders and GitHub-backed repositories that agents can use as context, along with metadata that makes future dispatches safer and easier to aim.

## Use it for

- Add or import repositories before asking agents to work on them.
- Set descriptions, tags, branch context, and automation preferences that explain how a workspace should be handled.
- Review git status and prepare operator-approved git actions without turning the Control Center into an invisible committer.

## Current guardrails

Workspace sync is explicit, additive, and reviewed before it scans. Pull and commit/push now show a review prompt with folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, untracked files, and the commit subject before changing files or pushing. Commit exits early when the preview sees no working changes. Project metadata save, status, delete, duplicate combine, repo create/link metadata, git actions, open-folder, and auto-commit mode changes fresh-read the tracker row before writing or using its path. Duplicate combine recomputes the duplicate groups from a fresh list and rechecks before save/remove. Auto-commit remains opt-in per project, throttled, and safe-state only: project cards show the current mode as a state chip, and mode changes live behind an Automation disclosure. Background checkpoints run only when the project row is unchanged, the repo has a linked remote, known compare state, no upstream-gone branch, zero ahead commits, zero behind commits, and working changes. Otherwise the checkpoint is skipped and the operator can review/request the commit manually.

## Good practice

Choose or update the project before sending code work through Chat or Work. A clear project focus reduces ambiguity for agents and makes attachments, uploads, and generated plans land in the expected workspace.

pages.body:
# Health

Health is the live fleet and usage view. It shows running state, token and model usage, provider activity, optional Headroom pilot readiness/policy, and the full agent roster through AgentTable.

## Use it for

- Confirm which agents are running, stopped, busy, or missing from the active fleet.
- Probe agent liveness, start or stop workers, rebuild where supported, and inspect team membership.
- Compare current token usage and model/runtime behavior before changing staffing or routing.
- Check active runtime credential-lane cooldowns and selected-agent rate-limit/failover state before assuming a model or lane is healthy.
- Check whether the optional local Headroom CLI/proxy is ready before choosing a pilot route.
- Draft pilot guardrails before a canary: Enable pilot route, Sampling, Workspace state, and the compact Trust & routing summary. Raw route/proxy/state path, Safety mode, protected categories, and validation gates live under Advanced.

## Current guardrails

Health no longer changes team coordinators directly. Lead stars are read-only markers sourced from the coordinator hierarchy, and the roster provides an Open HR Route handoff that lands on HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for coordinator and primary routing edits. Start, reset session, stop, rebuild, probe, and delete actions fresh-read the all-team fleet row before they mutate or probe the manager, then show current status plus before/after or effect summaries where the action changes process state. Runtime, model, effort, and speed dropdowns stage local draft changes instead of writing immediately; Apply changes reviews the full diff, verifies the live row still matches the staged baseline, rechecks after the confirmation prompt, blocks stale rows, and rebuilds each touched agent once. Start/Rebuild is blocked while that agent has staged config so old saved config cannot launch underneath the draft. Runtime credential-lane cooldowns are read from the manager as observation-only state: the roster shows an active cooldown count, warns on affected rows, and the detail panel exposes the selected agent's lane, cooldown/rate-limit, and failover metadata without offering a stale override button. The Headroom pilot card stages route, sampling, state, state-root, and safety changes locally; Apply policy reviews the diff before writing local pilot policy, and Refresh asks before discarding unsaved edits.

## Boundary

Health is optimized for operational status from the store snapshot and all-team aggregate poll. It is the current home for the roster grid that older specs placed on Dashboard, but it is not a long-term analytics warehouse or routing editor. Runtime cooldown/failover state is visibility only; lane rotation remains manager-owned. Coordinator and primary routing edits belong in HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync. The Headroom card stores local pilot policy only; it does not install Headroom, start a proxy, attach MCP servers, wrap agents, or promote compressed summaries into Brain facts.

# Identity & Keys

Identity & Keys is the review surface for agent identity, wallet posture, Safe-style account state, and scoped session keys. It helps operators understand who can act and what account or key material is associated with that action.

## Use it for

- Select an agent from the all-team roster and inspect identity status, account details, session keys, and warnings.
- Confirm wallet-bearing agents are visible even when they belong to a team other than the manager's active team.
- Review delegated capability posture before relying on an agent for identity-sensitive work.
- Keep onchain identity, wallet controller address, Safe account, and session-key concepts visible in one place.

## Wallet source

The page treats a valid controller wallet address as active identity evidence when it appears in OWS address metadata, SkillMesh address metadata, or an address-form OWS wallet field. Register and wallet-provision actions are routed through the selected agent's team so cross-team agents are resolved against the correct manager scope.

## Current guardrails

Renderer selection, proof state, backend key-provider account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are keyed by team/name. A controller challenge also includes the team in the signed message and cache key, so same-named agents in different teams cannot share a privileged proof. New challenge, verify signature, provision wallet, register identity, create account, deploy account, and issue session key fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before acting; revoke also fresh-reads the latest session row and blocks if the key changed or was already revoked. The UI only issues finite, spend-capped session keys and blocks full, uncapped, or non-expiring grants. When old bare-name mock accounts or sessions match current agents, the page now shows a Legacy Authority Review card instead of silently promoting or hiding them; scoped-agent selection and copy helpers guide a manual recreation path without trusting the old record.

## Safety note

Existing bare-name mock accounts are not automatically promoted into team-scoped accounts. The Legacy Authority Review card can select the matching scoped agent and copy the scoped authority target, but recreate, revoke, copy, or delete policy still stays explicit. Some provider behavior still depends on the active manager backend. Treat this page as the UI contract and verify the live provider before documenting stronger guarantees about signing, custody, non-expiring keys, or production-grade authorization.

pages.body:
# HR Manager

HR Manager is the organization design and routing workspace. It covers team structure, agent creation, roster management, instructions, and outbound relay topology.

## Owner

The legal-team `hr-manager` agent owns this page's staffing workflows, instruction-drafting behavior, and optimization proposals. Escalate legal-team policy or personnel-process questions through `legal/general-counsel`.

## Tab guide

- Structure: inspect the team graph and leadership shape. Shortcuts into Route now open the Relay policy mode directly.
- Build: draft and create agent rosters from blank text, recommended blueprints, manager templates, or saved configs.
- Manage: switch between Team ops for start/stop/probe/rebuild/delete and Instructions for persistent coordination directives.
- Route: switch between Overview, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync so relay edits, coordinator changes, and org sync are not stacked in one long pane.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator, primary lead, team relay, and per-agent relay writes fresh-read the relevant roster, hierarchy, or policy row before showing concrete before/after routing diffs. Primary lead promotion is locked to the default team; non-default teams can still set coordinators, but their controls cannot overwrite fleet-primary authority. Instruction Save & rebuild fresh-reads the selected agent and the current saved instruction text before writing, so a stale editor cannot overwrite a newer directive or rebuild the wrong moved agent. Selected-agent rebuild and move controls re-resolve the current team/agent row before acting, and empty-team delete rechecks that the team still exists and still has no agents.

Team lifecycle now uses one consistent current-state confirmation for Start, Stop, and Rebuild: it rechecks the team roster, shows current running/total counts, and blocks missing or empty teams. Team Builder fresh-reads the target team roster before onboarding; after agents are created it rechecks hierarchy and relay stamps before automatic team-coordinator wiring or relay policy writes, and never promotes a non-default team into the fleet-primary slot. The Build tab shows recommended blueprint coverage plus default return-path readiness, promotes default/lead to primary when building default leadership, and adds an explicit warning before non-default teams are built without default/lead, default/coder, default/researcher, and primary/coordinator routing in place. Builder coordinator presets also tell team leads to return substantial completed work through `default/coder` and `default/researcher` validation before `default/lead` treats it as final. Org sync also previews before apply; it fresh-reads org config and hierarchy, uses the same sidecar change detection as the real sync, shows scanned agents, goal files that would change, whether the brain hierarchy will be written, planned idle rebuilds under the rebuild cap, deferred rebuilds, and sample affected team/agent rows, then blocks apply if org state changed after preview. Query-busy and task-busy rebuild deferrals are team-aware where manager data includes team names, so same-named agents in different teams are less likely to block each other.

## Operating rule

Changes here affect staffing, instructions, delegation paths, and agent process state. Review generated drafts before saving, syncing, or rebuilding, and use the graph as a whole-fleet planning aid rather than a view of only the active team. Agent instruction editing is team-qualified in the picker and save path, so selecting an agent from another team loads, saves, and rebuilds that exact team/agent instead of silently falling back to the active team. Keep Team ops, Instructions, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync separated during daily operation so lifecycle buttons, text edits, relay writes, and org sync previews each have their own focused review surface. Owner-page handoffs from Dashboard, Health, Settings, and hidden Chat use App navigation:teams:route to land directly on Route > Hierarchy & sync. The shared team-coordinator instruction source now matches the default primary lead and secondary validator return path, but deployed coordinator-skill copies should be synced or rebuilt only after an explicit maintenance decision. The Build tab also ships app-side recommended blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams, shows present/total coverage for each, and keeps the default validator backbone visible so a downloaded IDACC has a useful first-run path even when the manager library is empty or older.

# Capabilities

Capabilities manages the extension surface available to teams, agents, and the Brain skill graph: MCP servers, skills, and plugins. It answers whether the fleet has the tools and instructions needed for a request before work is assigned.

## Use it for

- Inspect installed MCP servers, skills, bundled plugins, and Brain skill-catalog freshness.
- Attach or review capabilities for the selected team scope.
- Sync the local SKILL.md catalog into Brain so Brain dashboard Skills and Graph views can reason over the same skill definitions.
- Open Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, or Graph from the IDACC GUI without leaving the guarded dashboard-tab launcher.
- Confirm tool availability before assigning work that depends on a connector, library-specific instruction, or specialist workflow.

## Current guardrails

MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild now fresh-read every selected target before the confirmation prompt and again immediately before writing. The guard compares team, name, runtime, status, health, MCP metadata, and skill metadata, blocks stale or moved agents, excludes newly incompatible runtimes, and passes each resolved target's team explicitly so all-team or team-scoped actions cannot drift through the active manager team. MCP attach/detach still builds the replacement MCP list from the fresh metadata row, so a stale UI cannot erase another page's server attachment.

MCP registry Add fresh-reads the registry and treats same-name adds as an explicit replace. Catalog and custom replacement drafts now require Replace existing MCP server to be armed inline, show before/after endpoint details, and recheck the registry after confirmation before writing. MCP registry removal fresh-reads before delete, and library skill delete fresh-reads the current skill catalog before removing a skill folder. Skill creation still relies on manager-side validation and conflict rejection.

The Skills tab now shows local SKILL.md count separately from Brain's current /skills/index count and opens Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, or Graph through a fixed main-process whitelist instead of a free-form URL. Local create, delete, and manual re-categorize actions mark Brain catalog review needed until Preview & sync Brain succeeds; if Brain reports fewer skills than the local catalog, the same review strip appears without automatically mutating Brain. Preview & sync Brain fresh-reads local SKILL.md entries, auto-tag overlays, and Brain summary before showing the additive upsert/shared-memory boundary; it rechecks the local catalog after confirmation and sends a catalog stamp so the bridge blocks stale writes. The sync additively upserts local library skills into Brain's /skills/index graph and writes a shared catalog memory, but it does not delete Brain-only skill nodes or change which agents have a skill installed. Deleting a local skill therefore refreshes the shared catalog memory but intentionally leaves Brain-learned or previously synced nodes for graph review after the operator previews and applies sync.

## Scope note

Team scoping happens inside this page rather than only through the global footer selector. Keep interop fields explicit: MCP, OpenAPI, A2A, and signed-write discovery are separate concerns and should not be collapsed into one generic capability claim.

# Computer Use

Computer Use is the supervised local desktop-control broker. It shows the live display stream, permission state, pending approval prompts, and recent computer-use activity for agents that are allowed to request screen or input actions.

## Use it for

- Bless capable agents, arm or disarm control, and supervise the live stage.
- Approve or reject pending screen/input actions.
- Use pause, disarm, or panic controls quickly when local control should stop.

## Current guardrails

Blessing is scoped to the active team. The attached MCP config receives a team/name authority, the broker token map stores that scoped authority, and the arm-time bless list checks the same value before allowing screenshots or input. Old bare-name tokens are blocked by scoped arming and appear as a Legacy authority review notice near Who can drive, so operators can see stale access artifacts without automatically reauthorizing them. Review helpers can copy the scoped target and select the current agent for Bless, leaving the old token blocked.

Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and approval decisions now fresh-read broker or roster state before continuing. Arm re-reads the current attached-agent list before and after confirmation, then arms only that current list. Empty Arm for live-view permission testing now requires checking Start live view with no blessed agents before the Arm button enables. Bless fresh-reads the selected agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved or changed targets, and re-syncs the armed blessed list from current attachments after a successful rebuild. Remove re-reads the attached row before and after confirmation, detaches only the current scoped authority, rebuilds, and refreshes the armed list if needed. Allow/Deny re-reads the pending request and blocks stale or expired approval prompts. Disabling supervised per-action approval fresh-reads broker mode and still requires confirmation.

Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are not blocked by long attach/rebuild work. PANIC still disarms, clears pending approvals, releases held input, and records the stop in the broker audit.

## Safety boundary

Actions should run only through the armed broker and approval flow. The page depends on local OS permissions such as Screen Recording and Accessibility. Navigating away stops the live renderer view, although global notifications can still report relevant broker state. Conservative default: Arm with no blessed agents is still available for live-view or permission testing only after the empty-session review is armed; a stricter operator policy could require at least one blessed agent before arming.

pages.body:
# Settings

Settings contains local desktop configuration for hardware, manager connection, coordinator status, self-update, subscriptions, Ollama, image services, local stacks, EVM data RPCs, and inference backends.

## Use it for

- Fix manager connectivity or confirm the manager URL used by the app.
- Review subscription CLI state, local model providers, image generation services, EVM data endpoints, and inference defaults.
- Check for, stage, and relaunch into app updates.

## Current guardrails

Coordinator is now read-only on Settings. The Connection card shows the active team's current coordinator and hands routing changes to HR Manager, where coordinator and primary-lead writes run through hierarchy fresh-reads and before/after previews. Provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, same-name add/replace, local-discovery add, and Add all new now fresh-read the current provider list before writing. Disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked before and after confirmation. Same-name provider replacement now requires an inline Replace existing backend arm, shows before/after endpoint details, and rechecks the provider after confirmation; discovered-server bulk add re-runs discovery and de-dupes against the fresh provider list before adding anything.

EVM RPC save, replace, remove, and check fresh-read the current endpoint row before acting; replacing an existing endpoint shows before/after URLs. Local Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding the default Ollama backend. Local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current local inference limit before writing, and image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preferred local image generator before changing local-vs-cloud routing. Local LLM stacks now sort starter-friendly choices first and label install effort as start here, easy, guided, or advanced; Terminal install commands remain reviewed and explicit, with visible clipboard fallback when automation is blocked. First-run local setup now defaults manual backend add to Ollama, starts the local model pull field on qwen3:1.7b, and exposes a top readiness checkpoint for manager connectivity, explicit-vs-auto routing, local runtime readiness, ready backend counts, missing keys, and guarded quick actions for starter download, Ollama backend add, sync, explicit default, and local scan. Sign-out, stack install/uninstall, model removal, and provider removal remain explicit operator actions.

## Boundary

These settings are app-local configuration and infrastructure routing, not manager task data. Coordinator/routing edits belong in HR Manager, not Settings. Because release and update behavior is surfaced here, keep this page aligned with the package version and release process; the wiki drift check exists to catch stale release-facing documentation before shipping.

The hidden non-embedded Chat address panel also keeps coordinator marking read-only so it cannot bypass HR Manager if surfaced later.

Autopilot prompts show global scope, and Dream blocks duplicate nightly schedules.
