Workspace sync is explicit, commit/push and pull preview branch/remote/status/diff/untracked files, project-row writes fresh-read current tracker state, auto-commit mode changes sit behind an Automation disclosure, and background checkpoints only run when project and branch/remote state are exactly safe.

tabs.label: Health
tabs.description: Runtime/model/effort/speed selectors stage local drafts and apply only after full diff review plus fresh fleet-row checks before and after confirmation. Start, reset session, stop, rebuild, probe, and delete fresh-read the current row before changing context, process state, or liveness state. Lead stars are read-only hierarchy markers with an HR Manager handoff for coordinator routing. Runtime credential-lane cooldowns are surfaced as read-only manager state so operators can see why a lane is cooling without overriding the manager. Headroom pilot policy edits are staged until Apply policy reviews the diff.

tabs.label: Identity & Keys
tabs.description: Controller proof gates privileged actions; renderer selection, proof cache, backend account IDs, Safe/session actions, and the Tauri local mock key store are team/name keyed. Identity actions fresh-read the selected agent row, revoke fresh-reads the latest session row, and legacy bare-name key records surface as review-only evidence instead of being auto-promoted.

tabs.label: HR Manager
tabs.description: Coordinator changes, default-only primary lead promotion, relay overrides, instruction saves with rebuild, Team Builder auto-wiring, selected-agent rebuild, team lifecycle, empty-team delete, and org sync fresh-read current roster/config/hierarchy state before writing or rebuilding. Team Builder coordinator presets include the default coder/researcher validation return path, Manage/Route split risky controls into focused modes, the shared team-coordinator skill source teaches the same primary/secondary validation loop, and Build now has first-run recommended team blueprints.

tabs.label: Capabilities
tabs.description: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and rebuild fresh-read selected targets before and after confirmation; registry add/remove and skill delete fresh-read current catalogs before replacing or removing records. Same-name MCP registry replacement now needs an inline replacement arm plus post-confirm registry recheck. The Skills tab now shows local-vs-Brain skill-index status, flags local create/delete/re-categorize drift, and keeps additive Preview & sync Brain explicit.

tabs.label: Computer Use
tabs.description: Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode changes, and Allow/Deny now fresh-read broker, pending-approval, attached-agent, or roster state before continuing. Empty Arm for permission testing requires an explicit no-blessed-agent review before Arm enables. Tokens, attached-agent rows, and the arm bless-list use team/name scoped authority; legacy bare-name tokens surface as review-only evidence and stay blocked by scoped arming.

tabs.label: Settings
tabs.description: Provider default/toggle/remove/connect/add, local discovery adds, EVM RPC writes, local concurrency, and image-server preference changes now fresh-read current settings before writing. Same-name provider replacement needs an inline replacement arm before the final confirmation. Coordinator status is read-only here; HR Manager owns coordinator and primary routing edits. The last enabled inference backend cannot be disabled or removed.

controls.label: Preview before apply
controls.description: Use for org sync, git commit/push, capability bulk apply, and hierarchy/routing changes. The preview should show target agents/teams, files or repos touched, and whether a rebuild or push will happen.

controls.label: Stage then apply
controls.description: Use for compact dropdown-heavy config surfaces such as Health runtime/model/effort/speed. Draft changes locally, review the whole diff, block stale rows, and rebuild or write once per touched target.

controls.label: Fresh task preflight
controls.description: Use for task-card actions that mutate manager status, owner, or background dispatch. Refetch the task list plus lane/dependency overlays, block when status/owner/team/lane changed, and refresh before the operator tries again.

controls.label: Fresh plan preflight
controls.description: Use for brain-plan status writes, blocker-question creation, dispatch, and draft-plan promotion or deletion. Refetch live plan title/status/mtime or draft version/updatedAt/content before applying the action, and have the main writer reject stale expected brain-plan status snapshots.

controls.label: Fresh goal preflight
controls.description: Use for saved goal edits, AI refine saves, deletion, and driver metadata writes. Refetch the goal updatedAt marker before saving from an opened editor, and merge background driver metadata onto the latest saved goal instead of an older loaded copy.

controls.label: Fresh loop preflight
controls.description: Use for saved sequential loop chains. Keep the opened loop's updatedAt baseline, block save/run/schedule/delete when the saved chain changed, refresh the editor to the latest chain, and reload the saved record after each save so the baseline stays aligned.

controls.label: Fresh schedule preflight
controls.description: Use for heartbeat, schedule, check-in, and dream-schedule controls. Refetch current schedules or check-ins before update/pause/remove/close, block stale rendered rows, and reject duplicate recurring Dream schedules before creating another calendar check-in.

controls.label: Fresh project preflight
controls.description: Use for project tracker writes and filesystem/git actions. Capture the opened project row, refetch before saving status/metadata/delete/auto-commit, re-anchor git actions to the current path, and recompute duplicate groups before bulk save/remove.

controls.label: Project automation disclosure
controls.description: Use for background project automation such as auto-commit. Keep the card row read-only with a state chip, move mode changes into an explicit Automation disclosure, and retain fresh-project preflight plus safe-state checkpoint rules before any write or commit request.

controls.label: Fresh agent preflight
controls.description: Use for Health roster actions and staged agent config writes. Refetch the all-team fleet row before lifecycle, probe, lead, delete, and Apply actions; block if name/team/status/runtime/model/effort/speed/health/port changed before writing.

controls.label: Fresh identity preflight
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys privileged actions. Refetch the selected team/agent row before challenge, verify, wallet, account, deploy, and issue actions; refetch the latest account/session row before revoke so stale session state cannot disable the wrong grant.

controls.label: Fresh HR preflight
controls.description: Use for HR Manager staffing and routing writes. Refetch the target roster, hierarchy, relay policy, org config, or instruction text before coordinator/default-primary changes, relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, team lifecycle, Team Builder post-build team-coordinator wiring, empty-team delete, and org sync apply.

controls.label: Focused HR modes
controls.description: Split HR Manager Manage into Team ops and Instructions, and Route into Overview, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync so lifecycle buttons, instruction writes, relay edits, and org sync previews are not stacked in one surface.

controls.label: Coordinator instruction sync gate
controls.description: The central id-agents team-coordinator skill now matches the primary lead, secondary validator, parallel delegation, and goal-alignment policy. Deployed agent copies should be synced/rebuilt only after operator approval because that can touch live agent folders and processes.

controls.label: Downloadable team blueprints
controls.description: Keep first-run team creation useful even when the manager has no team library: IDACC provides safe spec-prefill blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams, and source changes reset stale roster drafts before parsing.

controls.label: Runtime cooldown observation
controls.description: Use when the manager owns runtime credential lanes, cooldowns, rate-limit failover, or provider recovery. Fetch current cooldown state, display affected lanes/agents as read-only operational truth, and avoid adding override controls until manager-side lane policy exposes a guarded write API.

controls.label: Manager response drain
controls.description: Use for every shared manager HTTP client and desktop bridge poll surface. Bound normal and long-poll requests with explicit timeouts, coalesce duplicate in-flight read calls, and even on 4xx/5xx or graceful older-manager fallback paths, consume or cancel response bodies before throwing so Electron and TUI runtimes release sockets instead of accumulating CLOSE_WAIT or SYN_SENT connections.

controls.label: Manager keepalive budget
controls.description: Use for the id-agents manager HTTP server. Cap idle keepalive, headers wait, and requests per socket so stale or pre-fix dashboard clients cannot hold thousands of loopback sockets forever; apply through a manager rebuild/restart maintenance window.

controls.label: Fresh capability preflight
controls.description: Use for Capabilities target writes. Refetch every selected team/agent before and after confirmation, block moved or newly incompatible agents, compare current MCP/skill metadata, and pass each resolved target team explicitly before MCP, skill, or rebuild writes.

controls.label: MCP registry replacement arm
controls.description: Use for same-name MCP server replacement. Detect catalog or custom drafts that collide with the rendered registry, show before/after endpoints, require Replace existing MCP server before enabling replacement, then fresh-read again after confirmation before mcp:add writes.

controls.label: Fresh Computer Use preflight
controls.description: Use for Computer Use Arm, Bless, Remove, safety-mode, and approval decisions. Refetch broker status, pending requests, attached agents, and selected roster rows before acting; recheck after confirmation when authority or rebuild state can change.

controls.label: Computer Use empty-arm review
controls.description: Use when Screen Recording is granted but no agents are blessed. Disable Arm until the operator explicitly acknowledges Start live view with no blessed agents; keep the broker preflight so a stale empty session cannot arm without review.

controls.label: Fresh Settings preflight
controls.description: Use for Settings provider, RPC, local concurrency, and image-server writes. Refetch the current settings row or manager value before writing, recheck after confirmation, and block stale replacement or last-enabled-backend mutations.

controls.label: Provider replacement arm
controls.description: Use for same-name inference backend replacement. Detect the rendered same-name provider in the add form, show before/after endpoints, require an explicit Replace existing backend arm before enabling the replace action, then still fresh-read and confirm before writing.

controls.label: Fresh read before full-list write
controls.description: Use for MCP server attach/detach and any API that posts a complete list. Refetch current agent metadata immediately before constructing the next list so stale UI state cannot erase another page's change.

controls.label: Team/id scoped authority
controls.description: Use for Identity & Keys and Computer Use so accounts, proofs, session grants, broker tokens, and blessed authority cannot collide when teams contain same-named agents. Legacy bare-name records should surface as review evidence until an explicit migration policy exists.

controls.label: Legacy authority review
controls.description: Use when older unscoped accounts, sessions, tokens, or permissions still exist. Show them as stale review artifacts, never as current authority, and require an operator policy before copying, revoking, or deleting them.

controls.label: Two-step destructive actions
controls.description: Use for reset session, rebuild active agents, provider removal, disabling the last usable backend, disabling Computer Use supervised mode, and stopping/removing recurring schedules.

controls.label: Observe-first Dashboard
controls.description: Keep Dashboard focused on observe, talk, refresh, and status. Move high-impact mutators such as primary-lead changes and org sync to HR Manager or require an explicit confirmation flow.

controls.label: Additive Brain catalog sync
controls.description: Use when an IDACC catalog feeds a Brain dashboard graph. Show the Brain count, make sync explicit, upsert current local definitions, and avoid deleting Brain-only learned nodes during refresh.

controls.label: Explicit Brain replay
controls.description: Use when a Brain dashboard needs compute-heavy POST analysis such as eval replay or vector comparison. Keep passive GET reports on timers, but run replay only from an explicit operator refresh and show when it has not been run in the current view.

controls.label: Same-name agent telemetry
controls.description: Use when Brain dashboards combine live all-team fleet rows with legacy name-keyed telemetry. Keep the live row visible, but mark SLA, cost, probe, or bare controller-link fallbacks ambiguous when multiple current teams share the same agent name.

routing.decisionStatus: The audit findings are surfaced. Fifty-nine conservative guardrail passes are implemented; remaining simplification and legacy-authority migration should wait for operator policy.
implementedFirstPass: Projects: stopped silent first-run workspace sync; commit/push, git pull, and enabling auto-commit now require explicit confirmation.
Projects: auto-commit remains opt-in per project and the automated checkpoint path reuses the reviewed enablement guardrail instead of prompting mid-background run.

Capabilities: default scope is now this team, broad or multi-agent applies require confirmation, MCP attach/detach refetches fresh agent metadata before posting a replacement list, and MCP registry removal confirms.
Computer Use: disabling supervised per-action approval now requires confirmation.
Settings: provider default changes, disabling enabled providers, and provider removal now require confirmation.
implementedSecondPass: Dashboard: drawer and command-palette project/org sync actions now confirm; primary lead selection routes by team/name and confirms before org sync.
Work: task lane status writes, owner reassignment, reconcile, redispatch, plan work/status changes, goal autopilot, schedules, loops, and dreams now confirm before live writes or dispatch.
Health: team lead, model/runtime/effort/speed, reset session, stop, and rebuild actions now confirm, with explicit active-agent warnings.

Identity & Keys: selected agent and controller proof state are team/name keyed in the renderer; wallet/account/session issue/deploy/revoke actions now confirm in addition to existing controller proof gates.

HR Manager: coordinator/primary changes, relay policy writes, per-agent relay overrides, instruction save+rebuild, selected-agent rebuild, Team Builder post-build wiring, org auto-sync toggles, and org sync now confirm.
implementedThirdPass: Identity & Keys: controller challenge messages, proof cache keys, key-provider account IDs, Safe/session deploy/issue/revoke calls, and the Tauri local mock key store now use team/name scoped authority.

Computer Use: attach/detach/attached methods route through the selected team, the MCP env carries ID_CU_TEAM and team/name ID_CU_AGENT authority, broker tokens store scoped authorities, and arm checks the same scoped bless-list.
Settings: coordinator changes now confirm, and disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked.
implementedFourthPass: HR Manager: Org sync now has an org:preview dry-run that uses the same sidecar change detection as org:sync and reports scanned agents, changed goal sidecars, planned brain write, planned rebuilds, rebuild cap, deferred rebuilds, and affected team/agent rows before apply.
HR Manager: Org sync task-busy and query-busy rebuild deferrals are now scoped by team where manager data exposes team names, reducing same-name cross-team rebuild ambiguity.
implementedFifthPass: Projects: manual commit/push now previews folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, untracked files, and commit subject before staging, committing, or pushing.
Projects: pull now previews folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stat, and untracked files before running the resilient pull.
Projects: manual commit exits early when the preview sees no working changes, avoiding a no-op commit attempt.
implementedSixthPass: HR Manager: coordinator changes now preview the previous coordinator and current primary before writing.
HR Manager: primary lead promotion now previews old primary, new primary, and affected team coordinator before writing.
HR Manager: team relay policy and per-agent relay overrides now preview previous and next delegation policy before writing.
implementedSeventhPass: Health: fleet lead, model, runtime, effort, speed, reset-session, stop, and rebuild confirmations now include current agent status plus before/after fields or effect summaries.

Health: Headroom pilot route, sampling, context threshold, workspace state, state-root, and safety-mode edits are staged locally; Apply policy previews the diff before writing, and Refresh asks before discarding unsaved policy edits.
Health: the Tauri adapter now honors the selected row's team for remote, probe, runtime, effort, and speed calls so all-team roster actions do not fall back to the active team.
implementedEighthPass: Dashboard: command-palette project/org sync commands now open Projects or HR Manager instead of mutating tracker or org state directly.
Dashboard: shortcuts drawer now defaults to observe actions and owner-page handoffs; direct project/org shortcuts live behind Advanced direct shortcuts.
Dashboard: direct org sync reuses org:preview before apply, and primary fleet-lead changes are no longer exposed as a Dashboard selector because HR Manager owns the routing diff preview.
implementedNinthPass: Projects: background auto-commit checkpoints now preflight git status and diff before drafting or committing.

Projects: auto-commit skips to manual review when the repo has no remote, unknown compare state, an upstream-gone branch, any behind commits, any ahead commits, unavailable diff data, or no working changes.
Projects: auto-commit enablement copy and tooltip now state that checkpoints only commit and push when the branch is exactly in sync with its remote.
implementedTenthPass: Identity & Keys: mock key providers now expose a read-only legacy authority report for old bare-name accounts and sessions that match current scoped agents.

Identity & Keys: the renderer shows a Legacy Authority Review card with account, deployment, active-session, non-expiring-session, and target scoped-authority details instead of silently promoting legacy key records.
Computer Use: the broker now exposes a secret-free legacy token report for old bare-name per-agent tokens that match current scoped agents.
Computer Use: the Who can drive panel now surfaces old bare-name tokens as blocked-by-scoped-arming review evidence instead of treating them as active authority.
implementedEleventhPass: Health: runtime, model, effort, and speed dropdowns now stage local config drafts instead of writing immediately.
Health: Apply changes previews the combined diff, verifies the live agent row still matches the staged name/status/runtime/model/effort/speed baseline, and blocks stale drafts before any write.
Health: applying staged config writes each changed field and rebuilds each touched agent once; Start/Rebuild is blocked while that agent has an unapplied config draft.
implementedTwelfthPass: Work: task lane drag/drop now refetches live tasks, lane overlays, and dependency overlays before writing a lane overlay or mapped manager status, blocking stale status/owner/team/lane cards.
Work: owner assignment now refetches the task and blocks if live status, owner, or team changed before overwriting ownership.
Work: individual and batch re-dispatch now fresh-read the task before reassigning or sending a new background request; stale batch rows are skipped and the board refreshes.
implementedThirteenthPass: Work Plans: Work, blocker scan, dispatch, Set pending, and Mark done fresh-read live brain-plan title/status/mtime before writing status, creating decision questions, or delegating work.
Work Plans: the main brain-plan status writer accepts an expected status/mtime snapshot and rejects stale writes if README status or plan-file mtime changed after review.
Work Plans: draft plan AI updates, promotion into brain plans, lifecycle status changes, and two-step delete now preflight the opened draft version/updatedAt/content before saving or removing.
implementedFourteenthPass: Work Goals: field edits, status changes, Autopilot toggles, AI refine saves, and two-step delete now refetch the opened goal updatedAt marker and block if the saved goal changed.
Work Goals: goal-driver config changes merge with freshly loaded driver config, and Autopilot enable/run-now prompts show the current global active Autopilot goal count and task cap.
Goal driver: background driver metadata saves now merge onto the latest saved goal record so driver notes cannot restore stale title/status/content from the copy loaded at run start.
Work Goals: removed the duplicate New Goal agent label so the form presents one clear agent row.
implementedFifteenthPass: Work Goals: after goal save/refine/field edit, the editor reloads the saved goal record so its freshness marker matches the store-written updatedAt value.
Work Loops: opening a saved chain now records a baseline and save/run/schedule operations block if that saved chain changed before the action applies.
Work Loops: run and schedule persist run metadata or schedule-ready definitions onto the freshly checked chain instead of an older opened copy.
Work Loops: saved-chain delete checks the rendered updatedAt marker and refreshes instead of deleting a chain that changed after the list rendered.
implementedSixteenthPass: Work Schedule: heartbeat enable/update now refetches current heartbeat rows before creating the replacement and pruning old schedules.
Work Schedule: heartbeat pause/resume/remove and other-team heartbeat controls refetch the schedule row and block if active state, interval, targets, or message changed.
Work Schedule: individual check-in close and stale-check-in cleanup refetch current check-in state and block when the active/stale set changed.
Work Dream: nightly dream scheduling checks for an existing 03:00 daily dream schedule for the same team/agent and passes the team explicitly when creating one.
Work Dream: dream report delete refetches the report and blocks if it disappeared or changed since the list rendered.
implementedSeventeenthPass: Projects: edit/save captures the opened project baseline, fresh-reads the tracker row before saving metadata, and preserves existing auto-commit settings during edits.
Projects: status changes, two-step delete, and auto-commit mode changes now refetch the current project row before writing.
Projects: manual commit, AI commit drafting, git actions, open-folder, and background auto-commit checkpoints re-anchor to the current project path before touching the filesystem.
Projects: repo create/link checks the project row before the GitHub side effect, then merges the resulting repo link into the latest tracker row instead of overwriting newer metadata.
Projects: duplicate combine recomputes duplicate groups from a fresh list, previews the group names, and rechecks the group fingerprint before save/remove.
implementedEighteenthPass: Health: staged runtime/model/effort/speed Apply now refetches the all-team fleet before the review prompt and again immediately before writing.
Health: team lead changes refetch the agent row and current coordinator hierarchy before confirming and writing coordinator routing.
Health: Start, Stop, Rebuild, Reset session, Probe, and Delete refetch the current agent row before issuing remote lifecycle commands.
Health: Start now confirms like the other lifecycle mutators, and Delete uses the richer status/effect confirmation instead of a bare one-line prompt.
Health: direct Probe buttons now route through the same fresh-agent preflight as the action menu.
implementedNineteenthPass: Identity & Keys: New challenge and Verify signature now fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before using controller wallet evidence.
Identity & Keys: Provision wallet, Register identity, Create account, Deploy account, and Issue scoped key fresh-read the selected agent row before mutating key or identity state.
Identity & Keys: Deploy also refreshes account state and blocks if the account was already deployed after the card rendered.
Identity & Keys: Revoke session key fresh-reads both selected agent state and the latest session row, blocking if the grant disappeared, changed, or was already revoked.
Identity & Keys: privileged button handlers now route through the guarded action functions instead of using stale selected name/team arguments captured from the rendered card.
implementedTwentiethPass: HR Manager: instruction Save & rebuild fresh-reads the target team/agent row and the current saved instruction text before writing, then rebuilds only that current agent.
HR Manager: selected-agent rebuild, move, empty-team delete, team relay, and per-agent relay controls fresh-read the relevant roster or policy row before prompting and writing.
HR Manager: coordinator and primary-lead changes fresh-read current hierarchy plus target agent existence before showing the routing diff prompt.
HR Manager: team lifecycle Start, Stop, and Rebuild now use one current-state confirmation with fresh running/total counts, and block missing or empty teams.
HR Manager: Team Builder fresh-reads the target team roster before onboarding and rechecks hierarchy/relay stamps before post-build coordinator or relay auto-wiring.
HR Manager: org auto-sync toggles and Preview & sync fresh-read org config and hierarchy; sync apply blocks if org state changed after preview.
implementedTwentyFirstPass: Capabilities: MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild now fresh-read every selected target before the confirmation prompt and again immediately before writing.
Capabilities: target preflight blocks moved agents, newly incompatible runtimes, and stale MCP/skill metadata, then passes each resolved target team explicitly to manager methods.
Capabilities: MCP attach/detach still constructs the replacement MCP server list from the fresh metadata row so wholesale MCP writes cannot erase newer attachments.
Capabilities: adding an MCP profile with an existing name now fresh-reads the registry and requires an explicit replace confirmation with before/after endpoint details.
Capabilities: MCP registry removal and library skill deletion fresh-read current catalogs before removing records.
implementedTwentySecondPass: Computer Use: Arm fresh-reads broker status and current attached agents before and after confirmation, blocking if the broker was armed or the blessed list changed during review.

Computer Use: Bless fresh-reads the selected active-team agent row and MCP metadata before and after confirmation, blocks moved/changed/incompatible targets, and re-syncs the armed blessed list from current attachments after a successful rebuild.

Computer Use: Remove fresh-reads the attached scoped authority before and after confirmation, detaches only the current row, rebuilds, and refreshes the armed blessed list if the broker is already armed.
Computer Use: Allow/Deny fresh-reads the pending request and blocks stale, changed, expired, or already-resolved approvals before releasing an action.
Computer Use: Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths and are no longer disabled by long attach/rebuild work; supervised-mode changes fresh-read broker mode before writing.
implementedTwentyThirdPass: Settings: provider default, enable/disable, remove, connect/sync, and same-name add/replace fresh-read the current provider row before writing and recheck after confirmation where routing changes.
Settings: disabling or removing the last enabled inference backend is blocked using the fresh provider list before and after confirmation.
Settings: discovered local-server Add and Add all new re-read the provider list, re-run discovery for bulk add, de-dupe by normalized base URL, and confirm the current bulk-add target list.
Settings: EVM RPC save/replace/remove/check fresh-read the current endpoint row before writing or probing, with before/after URL review for replacement.

Settings: local concurrency Apply fresh-reads the manager's current limit, image-server Save/Clear fresh-reads the current preference before changing local-vs-cloud routing, and Ollama auto-backend creation rechecks current providers before adding.
implementedTwentyFourthPass: Brain dashboard: /fleet-report now prefers live all-team manager polling over cached Brain agent entities.
Brain dashboard: Fleet and Agents tabs label live-manager versus cache fallback source and surface cache-drift warnings when Brain memory counts disagree with the live manager.
Brain dashboard: regression coverage verifies that live manager totals win over stale cached agent entities.
implementedTwentyFifthPass: HR Manager: fleet-primary promotion is locked to the default team in Structure and Route controls, while non-default teams can still choose team coordinators.

HR Manager: Team Builder post-build auto-wiring now calls coordinator:set for the target team instead of coordinator:setPrimary, so building an ops/research/engineering team cannot overwrite default-team primary authority.
HR Manager: Reactive org-sync copy now describes completed work flowing back through default-team validators, coder and researcher, before returning to the primary lead.
implementedTwentySixthPass: HR Manager: generic and roster-aware Team Builder coordinator presets now include a validation return path through default/coder and default/researcher before work is treated as final.
HR Manager: coordinator presets now tell team leads to ask coder for implementation/operations/code-quality validation and researcher for evidence/reasoning/sourcing/policy/completeness validation.
HR Manager: bounced validator feedback now routes back to the responsible teammate or team lead for another refinement cycle instead of letting unvalidated summaries reach default/lead.
implementedTwentySeventhPass: Settings: Local LLM stacks now carry install-ease labels so starter paths are distinguishable from advanced serving engines.
Settings: the local-stack shortlist now orders Ollama, LM Studio, Jan, GPT4All, llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, Docker Model Runner, vLLM, and TGI for a download-and-drive operator path.
Settings: reviewed stack install commands now show visible Terminal-open or clipboard-fallback status instead of failing silently when Terminal automation is blocked.
implementedTwentyEighthPass: Capabilities: Skills now displays Brain /skills/index status beside the local catalog.
Capabilities: Preview & sync Brain additively upserts local SKILL.md definitions into Brain graph nodes and writes a shared catalog memory.
Capabilities: Brain skill sync is explicit and non-destructive; it does not delete Brain-only learned skill nodes or change agent-installed skills.
implementedTwentyNinthPass: Brain dashboard Health: approval Approve, Reject, and Apply actions now fresh-read /approvals/:id before acting and block if the queue item changed status or disappeared.
Brain dashboard Health: Apply now confirms the exact approval kind and subject before mutating Brain memory, facts, instructions, or graph identity.
Brain dashboard Health: confirmation-required backend responses are handled by prompting for the required phrase and retrying explicitly instead of failing opaquely from a stale tile.
implementedThirtiethPass: Brain dashboard Learning: passive load and 30-second refresh now read learning report, metrics, and pending approvals only.
Brain dashboard Learning: eval replay and vector comparison now run only through an explicit Refresh eval replay button.
Brain dashboard Learning: route comparison starts with a visible not-run state and records the in-view replay run time or error instead of hiding recurring POST work behind dashboard polling.
implementedThirtyFirstPass: Brain dashboard Agents: duplicate current agent names across teams now keep live status/model/runtime visible but hold SLA, cost, probe-failure, and bare controller-link fallbacks as ambiguous.
Brain dashboard Agents: controller lookup now prefers strong scoped ids and refuses bare name or agent:name fallback when the same name exists in multiple teams.
Brain dashboard Graph: the graph app now states that filters, refresh, and JSON use read-only GET /graph/app/data and do not write Brain state.
recommendedFirstPass: Projects: decide whether auto-commit should remain as an explicit per-project feature or be removed from the UI.
Settings: decide whether same-name inference provider replacement should stay allowed with explicit before/after confirmation or require unique backend names.
Computer Use: decide whether Arm with no blessed agents should stay allowed after confirmation for live-view permission testing or be blocked until at least one agent is blessed.
Identity and Computer Use: decide whether legacy bare-name accounts/tokens should stay manual-review only or get an explicit migration assistant.
Identity and Computer Use: consider upgrading from team/name authority to immutable team/id authority if the manager exposes stable cross-team IDs for every shell.
routing.releaseState: This wiki page documents the current fifty-ninth guardrail pass and should ship with the next IDACC release after typecheck and wiki validation pass.
implementedThirtySecondPass: HR Manager: Manage now has focused Team ops and Instructions modes, keeping start/stop/probe/rebuild/delete controls separate from persistent instruction editing.
HR Manager: Route now has focused Overview, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync modes, keeping relay writes separate from coordinator and org-sync controls.
HR Manager: Structure Routing/Relay shortcuts and Routing overview Edit now open the Relay policy mode directly for the selected team.
implementedThirtyThirdPass: Agent instructions: the shared id-agents team-coordinator skill now teaches default/lead as primary, default/coder and default/researcher as secondary validators, parallel-first delegation, goal alignment, and validator bounce-back loops.
Agent instructions: deployed team-coordinator copies were found under live agent folders, so propagation is held behind an operator sync/rebuild decision instead of being applied silently.
Wiki: the guardrail audit now tracks the instruction-source update and the open approval gate for syncing deployed coordinator instructions.
implementedThirtyFourthPass: HR Manager Build: the Start from selector now includes app-side recommended blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams.
HR Manager Build: recommended blueprints prefill the spec and target team hint while preserving the existing review, AI-design, stale-roster preflight, and build confirmation flow.
HR Manager Build: changing any Start from source now clears stale manual or AI-generated roster rows before the new spec is parsed, so old rows cannot linger behind a new source selection.
implementedThirtyFifthPass: Health: IDACC clients, Electron bridge, and Tauri adapter now read /runtime/cooldowns from newer managers and fall back to an empty list on older managers.
Health: AgentTable shows an active runtime-cooldown count, warns on rows whose selected/current credential lane is cooling, and exposes selected-agent lane cooldown/rate-limit details.
Health: selected-agent detail now surfaces runtime lane and failover metadata as read-only manager state, folding in id-agents runtime lane/failover work without adding risky override controls.
implementedThirtySixthPass: Manager API: shared GET, POST, and DELETE handling now applies bounded request timeouts and drains manager error bodies before throwing, including older-manager fallback paths that intentionally catch ManagerError.

Manager API: response error parsing is centralized and long-poll event/query calls get a timeout window just beyond their manager wait value, so 4xx/5xx detail handling and hung calls no longer leave unread bodies or unbounded sockets on Electron/undici.

Desktop bridge: duplicate in-flight read-heavy calls such as agents, agents:allTeams, events, tasks:allTeams, query:poll, runtime:models, and runtime:cooldowns now share one manager request instead of opening parallel sockets for identical polling work.

Release hardening: this pass was triggered by a live installed IDACC process holding thousands of manager TCP sockets, so operators should quit/restart older running app instances to clear already-leaked descriptors after upgrading.
implementedThirtySeventhPass: id-agents manager source: the DB-backed manager HTTP server now sets keepAliveTimeout, headersTimeout, and maxRequestsPerSocket from bounded defaults or ID_MANAGER_* env overrides.
id-agents manager source: npm run build passes after the keepalive guard, proving the staged manager-side source and dist output compile with the current local manager changes.
Operational boundary: this manager-side keepalive guard is staged in the local id-agents repo and requires a manager rebuild/restart maintenance window before it affects the live manager process.
implementedThirtyEighthPass: Projects: project cards now show auto-commit as a read-only state chip instead of a row-level mode dropdown.
Projects: auto-commit mode changes now live behind an explicit Automation disclosure, reducing accidental background-automation toggles while preserving the guarded opt-in feature.
Projects: the existing fresh-project preflight, enable confirmation, re-baseline, and safe-state checkpoint rules still apply to every auto-commit mode change or background checkpoint.
implementedThirtyNinthPass: Settings: the inference backend add form now detects a same-name provider from the rendered fresh list and disables Replace until Replace existing backend is explicitly armed.
Settings: same-name replacement review shows before/after provider kind and endpoint details inline before the existing confirmation dialog runs.
Settings: changing provider catalog, kind, name, URL, or API key clears the replacement arm so an acknowledgment cannot carry across to a different backend draft.
implementedFortiethPass: Computer Use: when Screen Recording is granted but no agents are blessed, Arm is disabled until Start live view with no blessed agents is explicitly checked.
Computer Use: the arm handler also blocks stale empty-arm attempts if the no-blessed-agent review is not armed after the fresh attached-agent read.
Computer Use: the empty-arm acknowledgment resets automatically once Computer Use is armed or a blessed agent appears, so the permission-testing exception cannot carry into normal blessed sessions.
implementedFortyFirstPass: Capabilities: catalog and custom MCP add forms now detect same-name registry collisions and disable Replace until Replace existing MCP server is explicitly armed.
Capabilities: same-name MCP replacement review shows before/after endpoint details inline before the existing confirmation dialog runs.
Capabilities: MCP registry replacement now fresh-reads again after confirmation and blocks the write if the existing server changed during review.
implementedFortySecondPass: Capabilities: Skills now shows Local SKILL.md count separately from the Brain /skills/index count and labels behind, review, or extra-node states.
Capabilities: local skill create, delete, and manual re-categorize mark Brain catalog review needed and offer explicit Preview & sync Brain plus Graph actions without automatic Brain mutation.

Capabilities: successful Preview & sync Brain clears the local review state while preserving the existing additive boundary, so deleted local skills do not silently erase Brain-learned or previously synced skill nodes.
implementedFortyThirdPass: External Brain dashboard source: Skills now has a read-only /skills/index freshness strip with profile, generated time, IDACC library node count, and additive-graph boundary text.
External Brain dashboard source: the Catalog Overview now includes an IDACC library metric derived from idacc-library domain or skill-catalog tag facets.
Operational boundary: the Brain dashboard source patch is test-covered but live port 4200 will not show it until the Brain service is restarted or redeployed.
implementedFortyFourthPass: External Brain /fleet-report now exposes live, partial, or cache authority plus an authoritative boolean and status-authority label.
External Brain Fleet now changes the summary label to cached agent records, not live status, when manager polling falls back to Brain entities.
External Brain Agents now prefixes cache fallback statuses as cached and warns that cached rows are not live current-state proof before lifecycle decisions.
implementedFortyFifthPass: id-agents coordinator sources: operations and research lead YAML descriptions now state the default/coder plus default/researcher validation return path before default/lead finalizes substantial work.
id-agents SkillMesh coordinator sources: standalone ops, research, content, protocol, and combined skillmesh team leads now attach team-coordinator and carry the default validation return path.
Operational boundary: these id-agents config source changes are YAML-parse validated but not synced, deployed, rebuilt, or pushed into live agent working directories without operator approval.
implementedFortySixthPass: External Brain Health source: approval triage actions now reject stale queue snapshots before any Brain mutation and refresh the queue instead.
External Brain Health source: Approve, Reject, and Apply re-read the approval again after operator prompt or confirmation, blocking rows that changed while the review dialog was open.
Operational boundary: the Brain Health source patch is test-covered but live port 4200 will not show it until the Brain service is restarted or redeployed.
implementedFortySeventhPass: External Brain Learning source: passive GET report freshness is now labeled separately from manual eval replay freshness.
External Brain Learning source: route/vector comparison now states it uses only the manual eval replay snapshot and should be refreshed before routing or vector-rollout decisions.
Operational boundary: the Brain Learning source patch is test-covered but live port 4200 will not show it until the Brain service is restarted or redeployed.
implementedFiftyFourthPass: Settings: First-run readiness is now a top checkpoint instead of living only inside the Local models card.
Settings: the checkpoint labels manager connection, explicit default routing versus auto fallback, local starter/backend state, ready backend count, synced count, and missing key count.
Settings: quick actions reuse existing guarded flows for starter download, Ollama backend add, backend sync, explicit default, and local server scan.
implementedFiftyFifthPass: Capabilities: Skills now exposes Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, and Graph launch buttons instead of a Graph-only shortcut.
Desktop main: Brain dashboard opening is whitelisted by tab id and maps only to fixed localhost dashboard paths, preserving the no-arbitrary-URL boundary.
Capabilities: Markdown links to known Brain dashboard tabs now use the same whitelisted opener, so local docs cannot smuggle an unreviewed Brain URL into the desktop shell.
implementedFiftySixthPass: Settings: the Connection card now shows coordinator status read-only instead of exposing a dropdown that can write coordinator state directly.
Settings: routing changes now hand off to HR Manager, preserving the hierarchy fresh-read, before/after preview, and default-primary lock.
App shell: Settings receives the shared Router navigation callback, so owner-page handoff stays in-app without introducing a new IPC mutator.
implementedFiftySeventhPass: Chat: the non-embedded address panel no longer calls store.setCoordinator from the coordinator star.
Chat: the coordinator star is now a read-only marker while target selection still only changes the local chat session target.
Wiki: hidden Chat routing now documents that coordinator and primary-lead edits belong to HR Manager.
implementedFiftyEighthPass: Health: AgentTable lead stars are now read-only coordinator markers instead of buttons that call coordinator:set.
Health: the Fleet header now hands coordinator and primary routing changes to HR Manager through app navigation.
Renderer store: the generic setCoordinator helper was removed so non-HR pages cannot casually reintroduce coordinator writes through the shared store.
implementedFiftyNinthPass: App shell: the new teams:route navigation target opens HR Manager with a focused Route > Hierarchy & sync intent instead of dropping operators on the generic Structure tab.
Settings, Health, hidden Chat, the Dashboard drawer, and the command palette now send coordinator and org-sync review to the same focused HR routing pane.
HR Manager: Teams accepts the focused handoff, switches to Route, selects Hierarchy & sync, then clears the transient focus so ordinary HR navigation remains unchanged.

notes: Existing protections already found: delete task, delete project, combine duplicate projects, sign out, RPC removal, model removal, stack install/uninstall, team stop/rebuild, empty-team delete, Identity privileged actions, and Computer Use broker actions have confirmations or gates.
Most dangerous stale-write class found: UI computes a replacement state from a snapshot and sends a wholesale write, especially MCP server lists and git/project automation.

Most dangerous background automation found: project auto-commit can commit and push after task completion without another user click; the modified build makes enabling that behavior explicit per project.
Third guardrail pass closes same-name authority collisions for Identity & Keys and Computer Use without auto-migrating legacy bare-name records.
Fourth guardrail pass adds preview-before-apply to HR org sync and scopes org-sync busy checks by team where data is available.
Fifth guardrail pass adds preview-before-apply to Projects pull and manual commit/push.
Sixth guardrail pass adds before/after previews for HR hierarchy and relay routing writes.
Seventh guardrail pass adds richer Health agent-change previews, stages Headroom pilot policy writes behind Apply policy, and keeps Tauri all-team roster actions team-scoped.

Eighth guardrail pass makes Dashboard observe-first by routing palette mutations to owner pages, hiding direct sync shortcuts behind Advanced, requiring org preview from Dashboard, and moving primary-lead changes to HR Manager.
Ninth guardrail pass makes Projects auto-commit safe-state only and skips background checkpoints when branch/remote state is ambiguous or not exactly in sync.
Tenth guardrail pass surfaces legacy bare-name Identity key records and Computer Use tokens as review-only artifacts while keeping scoped team/name authority as the active path.
Eleventh guardrail pass stages Health runtime/model/effort/speed changes behind one Apply flow with stale-row checks and one rebuild per touched agent.
Twelfth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work task cards before lane, owner, or re-dispatch writes so stale board state cannot overwrite current task state.
Thirteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work plan records and adds backend expected-state rejection for brain-plan status writes.
Fourteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work goal records before goal edits/refine/delete and keeps goal-driver metadata saves from clobbering newer goal fields.
Fifteenth guardrail pass keeps Work goal freshness markers aligned after saves and fresh-reads saved loop chains before save/run/schedule/delete actions.
Sixteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Work schedule/check-in rows before heartbeat or supervision mutations and prevents duplicate nightly Dream schedules.
Seventeenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Projects tracker rows before metadata, status, delete, git, repo-link, duplicate-combine, and auto-commit writes.
Eighteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Health fleet rows before staged config, lifecycle, lead, probe, and delete actions.
Nineteenth guardrail pass fresh-reads Identity selected-agent and session-key rows before controller, wallet, account, deploy, issue, and revoke actions.
Twentieth guardrail pass fresh-reads HR Manager roster, hierarchy, relay, instruction, org config, and builder target state before staffing, routing, lifecycle, rebuild, and org-sync writes.
Twenty-first guardrail pass fresh-reads Capabilities targets and catalogs before MCP, skill, rebuild, registry replace, registry remove, and skill delete actions.
Twenty-second guardrail pass fresh-reads Computer Use broker, roster, attached-agent, and pending-approval state before arm, bless, remove, approval, and supervised-mode writes.
Twenty-third guardrail pass fresh-reads Settings provider, EVM RPC, local concurrency, image-server, discovered-server, and Ollama auto-backend state before infrastructure-routing writes.
Twenty-fourth guardrail pass makes the external Brain dashboard prefer live all-team manager fleet state over cached Brain agent entities, surfacing cache drift when the two disagree.
Twenty-fifth guardrail pass locks HR Manager fleet-primary promotion to the default team and changes Team Builder post-build wiring to set only the target team's coordinator.

Twenty-sixth guardrail pass updates HR Manager Team Builder coordinator presets so completed work returns through default/coder and default/researcher validation before default/lead receives final findings.
Twenty-seventh guardrail pass makes Settings local-model stack installation easier to reason about by prioritizing starter paths, labeling install effort, and surfacing Terminal fallback status.
Twenty-eighth guardrail pass makes the Capabilities Skills tab a Brain-aware skill catalog with a visible Brain graph count and an explicit additive Preview & sync Brain action.
Twenty-ninth guardrail pass makes Brain Health approval triage fresh-read queue rows before resolving or applying and handles backend confirmation-required apply responses explicitly.
Thirtieth guardrail pass moves Brain Learning eval replay and vector comparison out of automatic dashboard polling and behind an explicit Refresh eval replay action.
Thirty-first guardrail pass marks same-name Brain Agents SLA/controller fallbacks ambiguous and labels Brain Graph as a read-only GET view.

Thirty-second guardrail pass splits HR Manager Manage and Route into focused modes so lifecycle operations, instruction editing, relay policy, and hierarchy/org sync are easier to reason about without losing controls.

Thirty-third guardrail pass aligns the shared team-coordinator instruction source with the default primary lead, secondary validator, parallel delegation, and goal-alignment policy, while holding deployed-agent propagation for operator approval.

Thirty-fourth guardrail pass adds app-side recommended team blueprints and resets stale Team Builder roster drafts when the source changes, improving the downloaded first-run path without pretending manager-side templates exist.
Thirty-fifth guardrail pass surfaces runtime credential-lane cooldowns and failover metadata from the manager in Health as read-only operational state.
Thirty-sixth guardrail pass drains manager error responses, bounds request timeouts, and coalesces duplicate desktop read polls so stale fallback/error/hung paths cannot accumulate local TCP sockets.
Thirty-seventh guardrail pass stages an id-agents manager keepalive/socket budget and records the rebuild/restart boundary before it can affect the live manager.
Thirty-eighth guardrail pass moves Projects auto-commit mode changes behind an Automation disclosure while keeping the card row as a read-only state summary.
Thirty-ninth guardrail pass adds an inline Settings replacement arm before same-name inference backends can be replaced.
Fortieth guardrail pass requires an explicit Computer Use empty-arm review before starting the live view with no blessed agents.
Forty-first guardrail pass adds an inline Capabilities MCP registry replacement arm and post-confirm registry recheck before same-name server replacement.
Forty-second guardrail pass makes Capabilities local-vs-Brain skill catalog drift visible after local create/delete/re-categorize and keeps Brain sync explicit, additive, and non-destructive.

Forty-third guardrail pass stages a Brain dashboard Skills source/freshness strip that labels /skills/index, generated time, IDACC library node count, and the additive non-delete boundary before any Brain restart.
Forty-fourth guardrail pass stages Brain Fleet and Agents status-authority labels so Brain-cache fallback rows cannot masquerade as live current agent state.
Forty-fifth guardrail pass aligns active coordinator team YAML sources with the default primary/secondary validation loop while holding live /sync or rebuild propagation for operator approval.
Forty-sixth guardrail pass stages Brain Health post-prompt approval rechecks and stale queue snapshot blocking before any triage mutation.
Forty-seventh guardrail pass stages Brain Learning passive-report versus manual-replay freshness labels so old replay snapshots do not look current beside refreshed metrics.

Forty-eighth guardrail pass stages Brain Agents SLA coverage labels so unfetched telemetry cannot look like zero-cost/zero-failure health, and Brain Graph source authority labels so agent/entity statuses cannot masquerade as live lifecycle state.

Forty-ninth guardrail pass stages a read-only Brain Graph live-Fleet overlay that matches agent nodes only by internal id, scoped team/name, or unique bare name, leaving ambiguous and unmatched nodes cache-only.
Fiftieth guardrail pass makes Settings local-model setup more first-run ready by defaulting manual backend add to Ollama and surfacing model/backend/default readiness before provider changes.

Fifty-first guardrail pass makes HR Manager Build show preloaded-team coverage and default return-path readiness, promotes default/lead during default leadership builds, and confirms before non-default teams are built without the default validator backbone.

Fifty-second guardrail pass turns Capabilities Brain skill sync into a preview-and-apply flow with local catalog fresh-reads, post-confirm rechecks, and a bridge-side catalog stamp to block stale Brain writes.

Fifty-third guardrail pass adds manual legacy-authority migration aids to Identity & Keys and Computer Use: select scoped target and copy scoped authority without trusting, deleting, or auto-promoting old bare-name records.
Fifty-fourth guardrail pass lifts Settings first-run model/backend readiness into a top checkpoint with guarded quick actions and explicit auto-routing drift visibility.
Fifty-fifth guardrail pass turns the Capabilities Brain shortcut into a whitelisted six-tab Brain dashboard launcher.
Fifty-sixth guardrail pass removes the direct Settings coordinator write path and hands routing changes to HR Manager.
Fifty-seventh guardrail pass removes the hidden Chat coordinator-star write path and keeps Chat addressing target-only.
Fifty-eighth guardrail pass removes Health AgentTable coordinator writes and removes the generic renderer setCoordinator helper.
Fifty-ninth guardrail pass focuses HR routing handoffs on HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync so coordinator/org-sync review does not land on a stale generic tab.
The guardrail passes favor confirmation prompts and explicit disclosures over removing controls; the open UX decision is whether to replace confirmed shortcuts with preview-and-apply flows.
Most important simplification: separate status/observation pages from mutation/control surfaces wherever possible.
pages.body:
# Sync & Guardrails Audit

This page records the full pass over the dashboard pages in order: Dashboard, Work, Projects, Health, Identity & Keys, HR Manager, Capabilities, Computer Use, and Settings. It now documents the audit findings plus fifty-nine conservative guardrail implementation passes.

## Current build state

The dashboard family is sync-bus aware: dashboard, work, projects, health, brain-backed plans/goals/loops/schedules/dreams, agent capability changes, and org sync emit coarse domain invalidations so mounted pages refresh together after relevant mutations. The guardrail layer now reduces stale task-card writes, stale plan status/dispatch writes, stale goal edits, stale loop chain writes, stale schedule/check-in writes, stale project tracker writes, stale Health fleet-row writes, stale runtime credential-lane visibility drift, stale manager-client socket buildup after fallback/error/hung responses or duplicate polling reads, stale manager HTTP keepalive hoarding from old clients, stale Identity selected-agent/session writes, stale HR roster/hierarchy/relay/instruction/org-sync writes, stale Capabilities target/catalog writes, stale local-to-Brain skill catalog review drift, stale Brain skill sync writes, and accidental MCP registry replacements, stale Computer Use arm/bless/remove/approval writes and empty-arm sessions, stale Settings provider/RPC/concurrency/image-server writes and accidental same-name backend replacements, surprise mutations, same-name cross-team authority collisions, hidden legacy authority artifacts, unactionable legacy authority review rows, unreviewed org-sync sidecar/rebuild changes, unreviewed repository mutations, opaque hierarchy/routing edits, generic HR handoff drift, staged Health config drift, Dashboard shortcut mutations, unsafe background auto-commit states and accidental row-level automation toggles, Brain-dashboard cached fleet drift, non-authoritative Brain fleet cache fallback, non-default HR primary-lead overwrites, stale team-lead coordinator presets, flat local-model install choices, cloud-first Settings backend defaults, removed Settings, hidden Chat, and Health coordinator write paths, stale IDACC-to-Brain skill catalog drift, stale Brain Health approval triage actions, stale Brain Health post-prompt approval reviews, hidden Brain Learning eval replay polling, stale Brain Learning manual replay snapshots, same-name Brain Agents telemetry/controller ambiguity, partial Brain Agents SLA telemetry coverage, unclear Brain Graph read-only, agent-status authority, and live-Fleet overlay boundaries, unclear Brain Skills index source/freshness, Graph-only IDACC Brain launch affordances, crowded HR Manager manage/routing panes, stale shared coordinator instructions, stale team-config coordinator skill attachments, empty first-run team-template catalogs, and missing default validator backbones before non-default Team Builder runs without removing the existing owner-page workflows.

## Implemented guardrails

- Dashboard is observe-first by default: command-palette project/org sync entries open Projects or HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync, the shortcuts drawer shows observe actions and focused owner-page handoffs first, direct project/org shortcuts live behind Advanced, direct org sync previews affected agents/goals/brain/rebuilds before apply, primary-lead changes now belong to HR Manager's routing diff preview, and the hidden non-embedded Chat address panel can no longer write coordinator state.
- Brain dashboard fleet and agents tabs now treat the live manager as authoritative for current status: /fleet-report follows IDACC's all-team polling shape, exposes live/partial/cache authority, reports source/live-vs-cache drift, and labels Brain entity cache fallback as cached agent records rather than current live state. Brain Health approval triage now fresh-reads /approvals/:id before Approve, Reject, or Apply actions, blocks stale queue items and stale queue snapshots, re-reads again after operator prompt or confirmation before posting, confirms the exact approval kind/subject before apply, and handles backend confirmation-required responses explicitly. Brain Learning keeps passive report, metric, and approval refresh on the timer but runs eval replay and vector comparison only through an explicit Refresh eval replay action with visible not-run, run-time, error, passive-report freshness, and manual-replay snapshot freshness states. Brain Agents keeps live row status/model/runtime visible for same-name agents but marks SLA, cost, probe failures, and bare controller-link fallbacks ambiguous until scoped telemetry exists, marks unfetched telemetry as not fetched or unavailable instead of zero/healthy, and prefixes cache fallback rows as cached instead of treating them as lifecycle proof. Brain Graph now labels filters, refresh, and JSON as read-only GET /graph/app/data surfaces, emits a read-only source-authority contract, tags agent entity statuses as Brain graph snapshots that must be verified in Fleet or Agents before lifecycle decisions, and overlays live Fleet status only when a graph agent matches unambiguously by internal id, scoped team/name, or unique bare name. Ambiguous, unmatched, unavailable, or cache-only Fleet overlays remain labeled instead of overwriting Brain entity status. Brain Skills source now labels /skills/index profile, generated time, IDACC library node count, and the additive graph boundary; Brain Fleet and Agents source now label status authority; Brain Health source now blocks stale approval queue snapshots and rechecks approvals after operator review; Brain Learning source now labels passive report freshness separately from manual replay freshness, Brain Agents source now labels SLA coverage, and Brain Graph source now labels agent-status authority plus guarded live-Fleet overlay matching, but the live dashboard needs a Brain service restart before those source patches appear on port 4200.
- Work confirms task status writes, owner reassignment, reconcile, redispatch, plan work/status writes, goal autopilot, recurring schedules, loops, and dreams. Task lane, owner, and re-dispatch actions fresh-read live task status, owner, team, and lane before writing. Plan work, blocker scan, dispatch, status, update, promote, and delete paths fresh-read live brain/draft plan state before writing, with backend stale-snapshot rejection for brain-plan status writes. Goal edit/refine/delete paths fresh-read the saved goal updatedAt marker before writing, and goal-driver metadata saves merge onto the latest goal record. Saved loop chain save/run/schedule/delete paths block stale opened or listed loop records before writing or dispatching. Schedule heartbeat/check-in actions fresh-read rows before update/pause/remove/close, and Dream blocks duplicate nightly schedules plus stale report deletes.
- Projects no longer silently syncs the workspace on first open. Pull and manual commit/push now preview folder, branch, remote, compare target, ahead/behind state, dirty state, tracked diff stats, untracked files, and commit subject where relevant. Project metadata save, status, delete, duplicate combine, repo create/link metadata, git actions, open-folder, and auto-commit mode changes fresh-read the tracker row before writing or using its path. The row-level auto-commit dropdown is gone; project cards show the current mode as a state chip and expose mode changes only after opening Automation. Enabling auto-commit remains explicit per project; background auto-commit now preflights the current project row, git status, and diff, and skips to manual review unless the row is unchanged, the repo has a remote, known compare state, no upstream-gone branch, zero ahead commits, zero behind commits, and working changes.
- Health stages runtime/model/effort/speed changes locally, applies them only after a combined diff prompt, verifies the live row still matches the staged name/status/runtime/model/effort/speed baseline before and after confirmation, blocks stale drafts, and rebuilds each touched agent once. Lead stars are read-only hierarchy markers with an HR Manager handoff; start, reset session, stop, rebuild, probe, and delete actions fresh-read the current all-team fleet row before changing context, process state, or liveness state. Runtime credential-lane cooldowns and failover metadata are manager-owned read-only state in the roster, so operators can see active cooling without IDACC offering an unsafe lane override. Shared manager HTTP calls now apply bounded timeouts and drain error response bodies before throwing, and the desktop bridge coalesces duplicate in-flight read polls so dashboard/health/chat refresh loops do not open parallel manager sockets for identical work. The local id-agents manager source now also caps HTTP keepalive, headers wait, and requests per socket, but that manager-side guard requires a manager rebuild/restart before affecting the live process. Headroom pilot policy edits are staged locally and written only through Apply policy after a diff prompt. Tauri all-team roster actions preserve the selected row's team for remote, probe, runtime, effort, and speed calls.
- Identity & Keys now keys renderer selection, controller proof cache, backend account IDs, Safe/session actions, and Tauri mock key state by team/name, with controller proof gates and capped finite session-key enforcement still in place. Controller challenge/verify, wallet provisioning, identity registration, account creation/deploy, and session issue fresh-read the selected all-team agent row before acting; revoke also fresh-reads the latest session row before disabling a grant. Old bare-name mock accounts and sessions now appear in a Legacy Authority Review card instead of being silently promoted or hidden; the card can select the matching scoped agent and copy the scoped authority target for manual recreation.
- HR Manager now fresh-reads before staffing, routing, instruction, lifecycle, rebuild, Team Builder post-build, and org-sync writes. Coordinator and default-only primary changes check current hierarchy and agent existence before previewing diffs; non-default teams can set coordinators but cannot promote themselves into fleet-primary authority. Team and per-agent relay writes check current relay policy or override before saving; instruction Save & rebuild checks current agent state and saved instruction text; team lifecycle Start/Stop/Rebuild shows fresh running/total counts; Team Builder checks the target roster before onboarding, rechecks hierarchy/relay stamps before team-coordinator auto-wiring, and writes coordinator presets that return completed work through default/coder and default/researcher validation before default/lead gets final findings; org sync checks org config and hierarchy before preview and blocks apply if either changed after preview. Manage now separates Team ops from Instructions, and Route separates Overview, Relay policy, and Hierarchy & sync so operators are less likely to mix lifecycle, text, relay, and org-sync decisions. The shared id-agents team-coordinator skill source now matches the default primary/secondary validation loop and parallel-first delegation policy; operations, research, and SkillMesh coordinator team YAML sources now attach or describe that return path where missing; deployed copies and live manager config remain an explicit /sync or rebuild decision. The Build tab now provides app-side recommended blueprints for default leadership, operations, engineering, research, and onchain teams, resets stale roster drafts when the Start from source changes, shows coverage for each preloaded blueprint, promotes default/lead when building default leadership, and confirms before building non-default teams while the default validator return path is incomplete.
- Capabilities now fresh-reads every selected target before and after MCP attach/detach, skill install/uninstall, and target rebuild confirmation; the Skills tab can open Brain Fleet, Health, Skills, Learning, Agents, and Graph through a fixed tab whitelist; moved agents, newly incompatible runtimes, or changed MCP/skill metadata block the write. MCP attach/detach still constructs replacement MCP lists from fresh metadata and routes each action to the resolved team. MCP registry Add now requires inline Replace existing MCP server review for same-name replacement, confirms after a fresh registry read, and rechecks the registry after confirmation before writing. Registry removal plus library skill delete fresh-read current catalogs before removing records. The Skills tab now shows Local and Brain catalog counts separately, marks Brain catalog review needed after local create/delete/re-categorize or when Brain appears behind local, and keeps Brain sync explicit, additive, non-destructive, and previewed. Preview & sync fresh-reads local skills, auto-tags, and Brain summary before confirmation, rechecks local state afterward, and passes a catalog stamp to the bridge so stale Brain writes are blocked if the local catalog changed.
- Computer Use now fresh-reads broker status and attached-agent state before and after Arm confirmation; empty Arm for live-view permission testing is disabled until Start live view with no blessed agents is explicitly checked; Bless/Remove fresh-read the selected roster or attached row before and after confirmation; Allow/Deny fresh-read pending approvals before releasing an action; supervised-mode changes fresh-read broker mode before writing. Pause, Disarm, and PANIC remain immediate stop paths during long attach/rebuild work. Old bare-name per-agent tokens still appear as blocked legacy authority review evidence near Who can drive; the card can copy the scoped authority and select the matching current agent for the guarded Bless flow.
- Settings now fresh-reads provider rows before default/toggle/remove/connect/add replacement writes, blocks last-enabled-backend loss using the fresh list, rechecks discovered local servers before bulk add, fresh-reads EVM RPC rows before save/replace/remove/check, and guards local concurrency plus image-server preference writes against stale settings. Same-name backend replacement now shows an inline before/after review and requires Replace existing backend to be armed before the existing confirmation and fresh recheck can write. Local stack choices now put starter-friendly options first, label install effort, keep reviewed Terminal commands explicit, and surface clipboard fallback when Terminal automation is blocked. First-run local setup now defaults manual backend add to Ollama, starts the local model pull field on qwen3:1.7b, lifts manager connection, explicit-vs-auto routing, local runtime, backend readiness, missing keys, Scan running, Download starter, Add Ollama backend, Sync, and Make default actions into a top checkpoint before the deeper catalog, and keeps coordinator status read-only with a handoff that opens HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync for routing edits.
- Focused HR routing handoffs now use one App-shell `teams:route` target: Settings, Health, hidden Chat, the Dashboard drawer, and the command palette land directly on HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync, where coordinator and org-sync previews already have fresh-read guardrails.

## Remaining hard guardrail candidates

- Projects auto-commit now lives behind Automation; decide whether this guarded feature should remain or be removed from the UI entirely.
- Capabilities same-name MCP registry replacement now has an inline arm plus confirmation; decide whether guarded replacement should remain or unique MCP server names should be required.
- Settings same-name inference provider replacement now has an inline arm plus confirmation; decide whether guarded replacement should remain or unique backend names should be required.
- Computer Use empty Arm now requires explicit review; decide whether permission-testing empty arm should remain or be blocked until at least one current agent is blessed.
- Legacy bare-name Identity accounts and Computer Use tokens now have scoped-target helpers but remain manual-review only; decide whether to add explicit archive/revoke/delete cleanup flows.
- If stable immutable cross-team IDs become available everywhere, Identity and Computer Use should consider upgrading authority keys from team/name to team/id.

## UX simplification target

Dashboard should stay mostly observe, talk, and refresh. Work should stage or preview bulk redistribution and recurring behavior. Projects should separate tracker edits from repository-changing operations and keep background automation behind explicit disclosures. Health should keep runtime lane cooldowns/failover read-only unless the manager later exposes a guarded lane-policy write flow, and shared manager-client errors, hangs, duplicate polling reads, or stale client keepalive should release or share sockets before surfacing retry/fallback UI. HR Manager should own hierarchy and org-sync controls, with default-team primary authority kept distinct from per-team coordinator choice, lifecycle/instruction/relay/org-sync controls kept in focused modes, first-run team blueprints available without a manager template library, default validator readiness visible before non-default builds, and live instruction and team-config propagation treated as an operator-approved /sync or rebuild step. Capabilities should make bulk scope obvious before applying tools across teams, arm same-name MCP registry replacement inline, show local-vs-Brain skill catalog drift clearly, keep Brain catalog sync explicit/additive with stale-catalog stamps before Brain writes, and route Brain dashboard navigation through fixed tab ids rather than arbitrary URLs. Computer Use should reserve friction for privilege expansion and stale approval risk while keeping empty-arm permission testing explicit and stop controls immediate. Brain Health should require fresh queue reads and post-prompt rechecks for mutating triage actions, Brain Learning should keep replay/vector comparison explicit and freshness-labeled while passive reports continue refreshing, Brain Fleet and Agents should state whether rows are live, partial, or cache-only before operators compare them with IDACC, Brain Agents should refuse ambiguous name-keyed telemetry when same-name live rows exist and treat telemetry omissions as unknown, Brain Skills should label source/freshness before operators compare it with IDACC, and Brain Graph should stay read-only with agent-status authority and live-Fleet overlay matching labeled unless a future write flow gets its own guarded surface. Settings, hidden Chat, Health, and Dashboard shortcuts should keep coordinator/routing changes handed straight to HR Manager Route > Hierarchy & sync, while Settings keeps first-run model setup legible by separating starter local stacks from advanced serving engines, keeping local-first backend defaults visible, arming same-name backend replacement inline, and blocking impossible-to-run configurations.

## Open operator decisions

- Should Capabilities keep same-name MCP registry replacement with inline arm plus before/after confirmation, or block replacement and require a new server name?
- Should Capabilities keep Brain skill sync as an explicit preview-and-apply flow, add a post-create/delete prompt that opens that preview, or automatically sync local creates only while leaving deletes non-destructive?
- Should I restart/redeploy the Brain service so the staged Brain Skills source/freshness strip, Brain Fleet/Agents status-authority labels, Brain Agents SLA coverage labels, Brain Graph agent-status authority and live-Fleet overlay labels, Brain Health approval triage rechecks, and Brain Learning replay freshness labels appear on the live dashboard, or hold it for a maintenance window?
- Should Settings keep same-name inference provider replacement with inline arm plus before/after confirmation, or block replacement and require a new backend name?
- Should HR Manager keep guarded Team Builder coordinator/default-primary auto-wiring, move all routing wiring to a manual post-build step, or disable builder-driven routing changes entirely?
- Should non-default Team Builder runs remain a confirmed soft guard when the default validator backbone is incomplete, or become a hard block until default/lead, default/coder, default/researcher, and default primary routing are ready?
- Should HR Manager keep stamped org-sync auto-rebuild, make org sync write goals only with manual rebuilds, or disable org sync until manually re-enabled?
- Should I sync/rebuild deployed team-coordinator skill copies and updated coordinator team YAML sources across active teams now that the central instruction and config sources have been corrected, or keep them staged until a maintenance window?
- Should Projects keep auto-commit as a feature behind the Automation disclosure, or should it be removed from the UI?
- Should Computer Use keep reviewed empty Arm for live-view permission testing, or block Arm until at least one current agent is blessed?
- Should legacy bare-name Identity accounts and Computer Use tokens keep the new select/copy helpers only, or should IDACC offer explicit archive/revoke/delete cleanup flows?
- Should the id-agents manager keepalive guard be deployed now with a controlled manager rebuild/restart, or wait for a maintenance window?

pages.body:
# Wiki

Wiki is both the human documentation page and the editable schematic for the Control Center UI. Each article explains the operator purpose of a page; the structured sections below preserve tabs, tiles, fields, controls, displays, routes, and source files for implementation detail.

## Use it for

- Search pages, controls, routes, source files, and notes from inside the desktop app.
- Confirm how a UI surface is intended to behave before editing code or filing follow-up work.
- Keep app navigation metadata and human documentation in one reviewed JSON artifact.

## Editing rule

The desktop app hot-reload polls CONTROL_CENTER_WIKI.json through the main-process wiki bridge. Sidebar labels, icons, order, and visibility still come from pages[].nav for implemented routes, so documentation edits must preserve that metadata and keep every page.body non-empty.
